name: Cranberry Applesauce Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Put all ingredients in a machine for the sweet bread cycle, if available.
2. For the Cranapple Sauce: Peel and dice the apples into 1/2 inch pieces. In a large heavy saucepan, combine sugar and water. Cook over high heat until the sugar is dissolved. Add the diced apples and cook for about 5 minutes. Stir frequently. Add the cranberries and cook for an additional 15 to 20 minutes until the cranberries pop and the apples are soft. Using a potato masher, mash the mixture slightly (it will still be lumpy). Add the spices and zest; cook another 5 minutes for the flavors to blend. Allow to cool before using.
3. The cooled Cranapple sauce can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and serves 3 cups. It can also be served warm with dinner.
Note: This is a wet dough recipe.
Source: Diana Lewis (VGWN37A)
